an2.63:1.1:
"Bhikkhus, when, in regard to a disciplinary issue, the exchange of words between both parties,
the insolence about views, and the resentment, bitterness, and exasperation
are not settled internally,
it can be expected that this disciplinary issue will lead to acrimony and animosity for a long time, and the bhikkhus will not dwell at ease.
an2.63:1.2: "Bhikkhus, when, in regard to a disciplinary issue, the exchange of words between both parties, the insolence about views, and the resentment, bitterness, and exasperation are well settled internally, it can be expected that this disciplinary issue will not lead to acrimony and animosity for a long time, and the bhikkhus will dwell at ease."
